comments.author: @jonasthornander
comments.text:
One area where I believe Swedish society has fallen short is retirement planning. Many Swedes did not save or invest enough to secure a financially comfortable retirement, leading to the now familiar expression fattigpensionär.

For some, this was due to a lack of financial knowledge. Others placed too much trust in the welfare system to provide for them in retirement.

comments.author: @zainulabdin1720
comments.text:
Sweden is a truly amazing land of beauty, fascinating, warmth & incredible country Vikings to Fika.IKEA to Nobel...... 
 From the stunning capital city Stockholm to the historic skåne region, majestic north arctic Sami to charming Åre jamtland,  Kalmar castle to epic Gotland island 🏝️,
ski resort to world knows famous ice hotels, endless greeny forest to glorious lake (siljan), national parks to mount kebnekaise, wilderness of north to northern Greenlight.s
west cost & high cost is something out of this world. Every corner is like a masterpiece ❤ ppl are welcoming generous, passionate & kind- it is impossible not to feel the positive energy & gentleness of Sweden.
Sweden is a vibrant blend of art,medieval towns, unique culture, great music, full of history,green'energy, Innovation, equality, quality food, soul healing nature, liberty, iconic infrastructure,splendid countryside ,cleanliness,lagom,
snowfall,family oriented, ethical values, spirit of society,,, that's absolutely unforgettable. I believe Sweden got everything for everyone. 
Sweden isn't just a country it is a dream life experience that captures your heart & soul deeply. 
SEP 2026 election in Sweden 💫....
Greta thunberg ❤ queen of humanity....
No matter U stays with Sweden but Sweden will stay with you forever 💗 🇵🇰🇦🇪🇸🇪

comments.author: @DNA350ppm
comments.text: Regrettably the Swedish right wing parties thought that Swedish workers needed more international competition and so they wanted more cheap imported labor, and a much higher unemployment rate. In that quest the "national ideal" was bent from the existing less than one pro cent to a four (4%) unemployment rate - a dedicated goal set by then prime minister Carl Bildt. His and the like-minded cabinets were eager to push Sweden into the open-market European Union, with its over-national regulations. This was pushed hard, though some 70% of the Swedish people explicitly would have preferred just a more efficient Nordic collaboration (just between Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway, and Sweden).

comments.author: @DNA350ppm
comments.text: The paid pauses for fika mid-morning and mid-afternoon (coffee with reversed syllables: fee-co), parental leaves, work-life balance, free education, programs for housing for all, retirement pensions for all, regular benefits in case of sickness or unemployment - we got all that and more, thanks to very strong labor-unions, especially those run by our grand-parents, who weren't shy of taking to long strikes, if the employers didn't see reason. The labor unions were society builders with great goals, fighting for the strong foundation that makes Sweden livable today. The whole world can be grateful for their pioneering spirit and undeniable example. Free to be followed and implemented. World, up your sleeves for the common good! 🙂

comments.author: @katiewright2232
comments.text:
No intellectual discussion should ever say that the services are “free.”  There is no such thing as government funded. - the people who earned money fund everything through their tax burden. 
Whether a person desires to live with that kind of paternalistic oversight, allowing their hard earned money to be stolen from them for “good causes” or not, the truth remains the truth: taxation is theft  if anyone did it other then the entity we call the government, they would be considered a criminal for taking someone’s earnings by force  
Like it or not, that is a fact  
There is only one question to be answered: do you believe that one person has the right to own another person?
If you believe in an all powerful centralized government, then you believe that one person has the right to own another person (commonly noticed slave).  Even if the slavery looks pretty, as it does in Sweden, it is fundamentally what it is.

replies.author: @Timbermansthlm
replies.text: Taxes are  the most significant sign of a civilized society. Why do you think we Swedes don't mind paying taxes? The simple answer is that we don't worry about all the things that a majority of Americans worry about. That's why Sweden always rank among the best countries in the world to live in. What would it mean to you to not having to worry about expensive hospital bills, getting laid of, not being able to pay for your children's education and so on?

comments.author: @da206hbe
comments.text: Why do you suddenly show an US suburb at 23:20? Swedish houses does seldom have facades with horizontal planks. In towns and municipalites wires have been put under ground since the 70s. No pavements on the picture, at least not adjacent to the street. 😮

replies.author: @TOURGUIDE-05
replies.text: Good catch! That was an oversight during editing . The clip at 23:20 is indeed from a different location and not representative of Sweden. Appreciate you pointing it out.
